title: Compressibility Threshold for Incompressible Flow Analysis
summary: Incompressible flow analysis is satisfactory for gases and vapors at velocities below about 20 to 40 m/s, except in long conduits.

structured_payload.parameter_name: Velocity threshold for incompressible flow assumption

evidence_quote: Steady liquid flow may ordinarily be treated as incompressible, and incompressible flow analysis is satisfactory for gases and vapors at velocities below about 20 to 40 m/s, except in long conduits.

title: Ratio of Specific Heats for Air
summary: The ratio of specific heats at constant pressure and volume (k) is 1.4 for air and diatomic gases.
evidence_quote: k = cp/cv where k, the ratio of specific heats at constant pressure and volume, is 1.4 for air and diatomic gases.

title: Mach Number Threshold for Compressibility Effects
summary: When the upstream Mach number exceeds 0.2, the difference between compressible and incompressible flow results is significant.
evidence_quote: When the upstream Mach number exceeds 0.2, the difference is significant.

title: Critical Deep Body Temperature for Survival
summary: A deep body temperature (e.g., rectal temperature) below 35°C threatens loss of body temperature regulation, and 28°C is considered critical for survival.
evidence_quote: A drop in deep body temperature (e.g., rectal temperature) below 35°C threatens a loss of body temperature regulation, and 28°C is considered critical for survival, despite recorded survival from a deep body temperature of 18°C.

title: Lower Limit for Useful Outdoor Activity in Cold
summary: An ambient temperature of -35°C is the lower limit for useful outdoor activity, even with adequate insulative clothing.

structured_payload.parameter_name: Lower temperature limit for outdoor activity

evidence_quote: An ambient temperature of -35°C is the lower limit for useful outdoor activity, even with adequate insulative clothing.

title: Maximum Heat Production from Shivering
summary: Violent, whole-body shivering causes maximum heat production of about 6 times resting levels.
evidence_quote: Ultimately violent, whole-body shivering causes maximum heat production of about 6 times resting levels, rendering the individual totally ineffective.

title: Damper Leakage Concern for Energy and Freeze Prevention
summary: Damper leakage is a concern for tight shutoff to reduce energy consumption; outdoor air dampers in cold climates must close tightly to prevent coils and pipes from freezing.
evidence_quote: Damper leakage is a concern, particularly where tight shutoff is necessary to significantly reduce energy consumption. Also, outdoor air dampers in cold climates must close tightly to prevent coils and pipes from freezing.

title: Standard Electronic Control Signals for Dampers
summary: Standard electronic signals from controllers or DDC systems, such as 4 to 20 mA, 2 to 10 V (dc), or 0 to 10 V (dc), can be used to control proportionally actuated dampers.
evidence_quote: In addition, a variety of standard electronic signals from electronic controllers or DDC systems, such as 4 to 20 mA, 2 to 10 V (dc), or 0 to 10 V (dc), can be used to control proportionally actuated dampers.

title: Damper Actuator Mounting Recommendation
summary: Mounting methods that link the actuator to a single blade are not recommended because of the potential to bend the single drive blade or to have the blade-to-blade linkages get out of adjustment.
evidence_quote: Mounting methods that link the actuator to a single blade are not recommended because of the potential to bend the single drive blade or to have the blade-to-blade linkages get out of adjustment.

title: Rank-Order Assessment for Microorganism Air Sampling
summary: Rank-order assessment lists individual organisms in descending order of abundance for a complainant indoor site and control locations; predominance of a microbe in the complainant site but not controls suggests a source.
evidence_quote: Individual organisms are listed in descending order of abundance for a complainant indoor site and for one or more control locations. The predominance of one or more microbes in the complainant site, but not in the control sites or outdoors, suggests the presence of a source for that organism.

title: Real-Time PCR Detection Time
summary: Real-time PCR can often be run in 10 to 24 hours, compared to 3 to 7 days for traditional culturing.
evidence_quote: Traditional culturing can take anywhere from 3 to 7 days, whereas real-time PCR can often be run in 10 to 24 h (Yang and Rothman 2004).

title: Methods for Controlling Airborne Particulate Levels
summary: Control of airborne particulate levels may be achieved by reduction of source emissions, capture at source using local exhaust, dilution using mechanical ventilation, or removal from ventilation air by filtration.
evidence_quote: Control of airborne particulate levels may be achieved by one of four methods: Reduction of source emissions, Capture of emissions at the source using local exhaust, Dilution using mechanical ventilation, Removal from ventilation air by filtration.

title: Definition of Gas vs. Vapor
summary: Gas is the correct term for any pure substance or mixture that naturally exists in the gaseous state at normal atmospheric conditions (vapor pressure > ambient pressure). Vapor describes a substance in the gaseous state whose natural state is a liquid or solid (vapor pressure < ambient pressure).
evidence_quote: Gas is the correct term for describing any pure substance or mixture that naturally exists in the gaseous state at normal atmospheric conditions. That is, its vapor pressure is greater than ambient pressure at ambient temperature. Vapor is used to describe a substance in the gaseous state whose natural state is a liquid or solid at normal atmospheric conditions. The vapor pressure is below ambient pressure at ambient temperature.
